preamble: The raising of the near-open front unrounded vowel [æ] all the way to the close front unrounded vowel [i] is an extreme case of vowel raising, often occurring in chain shifts or intensive dialectal fronting.

examples.note: Intensive raising of [æ] in certain phonetic contexts, often with breaking.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
